headline: Sennheiser launches new Momentum 5 Wireless headphones.
summary: Features upgraded Active Noise Cancellation. Now includes a user-replaceable battery. Maintains a contemporary design similar to its predecessor.

headline: Ferrari unveils its first EV, the Luce, with Jony Ive's design touch.
summary: The Luce marks Ferrari's inaugural electric vehicle. Designed in collaboration with Jony Ive's LoveFrom studio. It's also Ferrari's second four-door model.

headline: Cox Media fined by FTC for falsely claiming to spy on phone users.
summary: Cox Media and marketing firms were penalized for deceptive practices. They boasted about secretly listening to users via devices. The companies will pay a substantial settlement.

headline: Best Memorial Day sales offering deals on tech and gadgets.
summary: Memorial Day sales are now active with numerous discounts. Find great prices on portable speakers and solar lights. Excellent opportunity to upgrade your tech for the season.

headline: Bungie staff reportedly unaware of Destiny 2 support changes.
summary: Most Bungie employees were reportedly not informed about Destiny 2 plans. News of support discontinuation was public before internal notification. This raises concerns about internal communication at Bungie.

headline: New 2026 Apple iPad Air with M4 chip hits record low price.
summary: The latest iPad Air (2026) with M4 chip is significantly discounted. It reached an all-time low price during Memorial Day sales. Save $80 compared to buying directly from Apple.

headline: Tekken director Katsuhiro Harada finally visits Waffle House.
summary: Katsuhiro Harada, Tekken's director, made his long-anticipated trip to Waffle House. Fans have eagerly followed his past discussions about the restaurant. He shared the experience, delighting the gaming community.

headline: LittleBigPlanet dev Media Molecule's next game rumored to be open-world.
summary: Media Molecule, known for LittleBigPlanet and Dreams, is developing a new title. Reports suggest the upcoming game will feature open-world elements. This indicates a potential new direction for the studio.

headline: Gisèle Pelicot "deeply shocked" by decision in rape case.
summary: French rape survivor Gisèle Pelicot voiced profound shock. She criticized the decision not to jail boys in a Fordingbridge rape case. Pelicot praised the young girl who bravely came forward.

headline: Top doctors warn social media as harmful as smoking for youth.
summary: The Academy of Medical Royal Colleges highlights social media's dangers. Doctors are advised to routinely check screen time and social media use. The warning underscores significant health concerns for younger patients.

headline: US launches new strikes on Iran, targeting missile sites and boats.
summary: US Central Command announced self-defense strikes against Iran. Targets included key missile sites and maritime assets. The action comes amidst peace talks in Qatar.

headline: Next boss warns of "dramatic" fall in entry-level job opportunities.
summary: Lord Wolfson of Next Plc reported a sharp decline in entry-level jobs. Applicant numbers have doubled per role in two years. This signals increasing competition and a tough job market for new entrants.
